records.ety: [L. Martius mensis Mars'month fr. Martius belonging to Mars, the god of war: cf. F. mars. Cf. Martial.]

defs: The third month of the year, containing thirty-one days.
quotes: The stormy March is come at last, With wind, and cloud, and changing skies.

records.ety: [OE. marche, F. marche; of German origin; cf. OHG. marcha, G. mark, akin to OS. marka, AS. mearc, Goth. marka, L. margo edge, border, margin, and possibly to E. mark a sign. √106. Cf. Margin, Margrave, Marque, Marquis.]

defs: A territorial border or frontier; a region adjacent to a boundary line; a confine; -- used chiefly in the plural, and in English history applied especially to the border land on the frontiers between England and Scotland, and England and Wales.
quotes: Geneva is situated in the marches of several dominions -- France, Savoy, and Switzerland.
Lords of waste marches, kings of desolate isles.

defs: To border; to be contiguous; to lie side by side.
quotes: That was in a strange land Which marcheth upon Chimerie.

records.ety: [F. marcher, in OF. also, to tread, prob. fr. L. marcus hammer. Cf. Mortar.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Marched (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Marching.]

defs: To move with regular steps, as a soldier; to walk in a grave, deliberate, or stately manner; to advance steadily.
To proceed by walking in a body or in military order; as, the German army marched into France.

To cause to move with regular steps in the manner of a soldier; to cause to move in military array, or in a body, as troops; to cause to advance in a steady, regular, or stately manner; to cause to go by peremptory command, or by force.
quotes: March them again in fair array.

defs: The act of marching; a movement of soldiers from one stopping place to another; military progress; advance of troops.
quotes: These troops came to the army harassed with a long and wearisome march.

defs: Hence: Measured and regular advance or movement, like that of soldiers moving in order; stately or deliberate walk; steady onward movement; as, the march of time.
quotes: With solemn march Goes slow and stately by them.
This happens merely because men will not bide their time, but will insist on precipitating the march of affairs.

defs: The distance passed over in marching; as, an hour's march; a march of twenty miles.
A piece of music designed or fitted to accompany and guide the movement of troops; a piece of music in the march form.
quotes: The drums presently striking up a march.
